signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH is the cleanest constructive major, but not enough to justify a fresh long while veto is active.
SOL has some constructive tone, but not enough to overcome the event brake.
DASH remains a decent watch-only long, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket.
VVV has improving squeeze-long traits with negative funding, but still not enough to justify fresh risk under veto.
BABY has a mildly constructive setup now, but it remains too catalyst-distorted and too thin to trust as a fresh long.
STBL remains the clearest bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the upside still looks vulnerable.
ZEN has some momentum, but not enough structural edge to justify action here.
LIT has momentum but not enough clean edge to justify a fresh trade.
TNSR remains a valid bearish continuation because both price and mechanics are still rolling lower.
HEMI has some movement, but not enough clean edge to justify action while veto is active.
